title: Enforce explicit Morty tool-scope permissions in delegation prompts
source_context: /insights: 'Parallel Mortys launched without Edit/Write permissions returned empty, forcing Claude to redo all fixes directly after the rebase approach also failed'. Our agent JSON files don't declare tool scopes. [PRIORITY: /insights report identified as top-ROI fix]
description: From /insights: parallel Morty teams repeatedly stalled because sub-agents launched without Edit/Write/Bash permissions, forcing Rick to redo work manually. Currently delegate.py's build_prompt implicitly assumes Morty's have full tool access. Fix: for each Morty role, explicitly enumerate its required tool scope in the system prompt (e.g., backend-morty: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob]; security-morty: [Read, Grep, Bash-audit-only]). Pass this as an explicit 'ALLOWED TOOLS' block so the CLI/sandbox layer grants the right scope. Also document this in references/team-templates.md.
proposed_changes: Add 'allowed_tools' field to each agents/*-morty.json. In build_prompt, inject: '## ALLOWED TOOLS\nYou have permission to use: {tools}. Do not attempt tools outside this list.' Also surface in the console preview so users see what the Morty will be able to do.
